category_title: Older preschool children expressing specific verbal and emotional concerns about transition
minimal_context: The teacher and student are having an early conversation in the classroom about the upcoming move to a new building.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Today we are going to have a conversation about moving to the new school building.

STUDENT: I am worried about our classroom.

TUTOR: What are you thinking about?

STUDENT: I am looking at all of our materials in here.

TUTOR: We will be moving soon.

STUDENT: Will the globe go? Will the blocks go? Will the tire swing go?

options.A: Addresses the repetitive questions about the classroom materials once, and then does not allow the questioning behavior to continue during the rest of the conversation.
options.B: Encourages the student to select items to bring to the new school by placing sticky notes on the materials, and then helps them make a list.
options.C: Works on the student's recognition of body language and facial cues during the conversation about the move while, at the same time, explicitly labeling feelings.
options.D: Uses a 'request for clarification' during the conversation about the move, asking the student for further explanations and elaborations regarding the classroom materials.
